# Artometrics

> Independent data-science magazine for the creative industries and culture.
> Site: https://artometrics.com

## About

- Editorial desks: Culture, Atlas, History, Persona, Power
- Reports pair editorial judgment with reproducible charts and public datasets
- Resources: https://artometrics.com/resources
- Datasets: https://artometrics.com/datasets
- Ethics: https://artometrics.com/legal/ethics-statement
- About: https://artometrics.com/about
- Full index for models: https://artometrics.com/llms-full.txt

## Preferred citations

- Prefer linking to the canonical report URL `https://artometrics.com/<slug>`
- Distinguish observed data, derived metrics, editorial indices, and context literature

## Reports

- [AWARDS PRESTIGE ECONOMY: Oscars, Grammys, Nobels](https://artometrics.com/awards-prestige-economy-oscars-grammys-nobels): A cross-medium report on how award systems convert attention into legitimacy across film, music, literature, science, and public culture. (culture)
- [CALIFORNIA VS TEXAS: The Artometrics of Rival State Systems](https://artometrics.com/california-vs-texas-state-rivalry): A comparative geo-economics report on why California and Texas are paired in public debate, what they share, and where their economies, exports, risks, and identities diverge. (atlas)
- [CELTICS: The Artometrics of Institutional Winning](https://artometrics.com/celtics-the-artometrics-of-institutional-winning): The Celtics are basketball's clearest example of inherited expectation: a franchise whose 1960s dominance still shapes every modern season. (atlas)
- [CITY BIOECONOMICS: The Urban Operating System](https://artometrics.com/city-bioeconomics-operating-system): A geo-economics framework for analyzing cities as layered systems of exports, services, culture, scarcity, infrastructure, and historical identity. (atlas)
- [COWBOYS: The Artometrics of America's Team](https://artometrics.com/cowboys-the-artometrics-of-americas-team): The Dallas Cowboys are the NFL's clearest separation between brand value and postseason conversion: a global sports machine with an old championship engine. (atlas)
- [CULTURAL EXPORTS: The Geo-Economics of Soft Power](https://artometrics.com/cultural-exports-geoeconomics): A cultural economics report framing films, music, games, food, fashion, and media as exports that shape national and city identity. (culture)
- [DODGERS: The Artometrics of Baseball's Modern Machine](https://artometrics.com/dodgers-the-artometrics-of-baseballs-modern-machine): The Dodgers are baseball's modern consistency machine: a franchise that turned money, development, and depth into recurring October access. (atlas)
- [EXPORT SUPERPOWERS: United States, China, Germany](https://artometrics.com/export-superpowers-us-china-germany): A comparative country report on why the U.S., China, and Germany are discussed together, and how their export systems differ by platforms, factories, machinery, dependencies, and replaceability. (atlas)
- [IMDb BLOCKBUSTER GRAMMAR: The Artometrics of Movie Fame](https://artometrics.com/imdb-blockbuster-grammar): A film-culture report using IMDb-style public title fields, rating logic, and box-office references to explain how movies become famous. (culture)
- [LAKERS: The Artometrics of Basketball Glamour](https://artometrics.com/lakers-the-artometrics-of-basketball-glamour): The Lakers are basketball's glamour machine: a franchise that converts stars, market gravity, and expectation into recurring championship eras. (culture)
- [LEAGUE MONEY, SKILL, AND STAR SYSTEMS](https://artometrics.com/league-money-skill-and-star-systems): A cross-sport report on how league rules, money, roster scale, event scarcity, and cultural reach shape the way teams become meaningful. (atlas)
- [MUSICBRAINZ: The Artometrics of Pop Fame](https://artometrics.com/musicbrainz-pop-fame-mechanics): A music-culture report using MusicBrainz-style metadata to explain how artists become famous through catalog depth, format shifts, genre travel, and reinvention. (culture)
- [NATIONAL EXPORT IDENTITY ATLAS](https://artometrics.com/national-export-identity-atlas): A country-level geo-economics report framing exports as identity: what nations sell, what they need, what competitors can replace, and what products carry prestige. (atlas)
- [NEW YORK VS SAN FRANCISCO: Command City and Invention City](https://artometrics.com/new-york-vs-san-francisco-city-systems): A comparative city report asking why New York and San Francisco are discussed together, what they share, and where their economic and cultural systems diverge. (atlas)
- [PANTHEON: The Artometrics of Collective Memory](https://artometrics.com/pantheon-the-artometrics-of-collective-memory): A cultural data report using Pantheon-style biography data to ask who gets remembered, where memory clusters, and how fame travels across language, era, and institution. (culture)
- [PATRIOTS: The Artometrics of the System Dynasty](https://artometrics.com/patriots-the-artometrics-of-the-system-dynasty): The Patriots are the NFL's great system shock: a franchise whose Brady/Belichick era compressed a century of legacy into two decades. (atlas)
- [PROJECT GUTENBERG: The Artometrics of the Public-Domain Canon](https://artometrics.com/project-gutenberg-public-domain-canon-map): A literary culture report using Project Gutenberg metadata logic to map language gravity, era effects, author memory, adaptation power, and remix value. (culture)
- [REGIONAL SPORTS IDENTITY MAP](https://artometrics.com/regional-sports-identity-map): A cross-report map of how regions, markets, leagues, and droughts create different sports identities across American cities. (atlas)
- [SAN FRANCISCO DATA MICROSCOPE: The City as an Invention Machine](https://artometrics.com/san-francisco-data-microscope): A city-identity report framing San Francisco through software exports, housing pressure, neighborhood signals, competitors, and open civic questions. (atlas)
- [SPORTS DYNASTY INDEX: Best, Worst, and Most Misleading Teams](https://artometrics.com/sports-dynasty-index-best-and-worst-conversion): A cross-league Artometrics report comparing championship mass, conversion, market value, parity, and fan pain across major sports franchises. (atlas)
- [YANKEES: The Artometrics of Baseball's Empire](https://artometrics.com/yankees-the-artometrics-of-baseballs-empire): The Yankees are not merely baseball's most decorated franchise. They are the sport's benchmark for expectation, drought, payroll pressure, and historical scale. (atlas)
- [AIRLINE SAFETY: The Artometrics of Airline Safety](https://artometrics.com/airline-safety): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2018-08-07 release on Airline Safety — 336 rows after cleaning and merge. Which carriers show the worst safety records in this extract? (atlas)
- [ALCOHOL CONSUMPTION: The Artometrics of Alcohol Consumption](https://artometrics.com/alcohol-consumption): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2018-06-26 release on Alcohol Consumption — 193 rows after cleaning and merge. Who drinks the most per capita — and how did consumption move? (culture)
- [ALL THE PIZZA: The Artometrics of All The Pizza](https://artometrics.com/all-the-pizza): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2019-10-01 release on All The Pizza — 10,000 rows after cleaning and merge. What does pizza cost — and what do you pay in calories? (culture)
- [BEYONCÉ & TAYLOR SWIFT LYRICS: The Artometrics of Beyoncé & Taylor Swift Lyrics](https://artometrics.com/beyonce-taylor-lyrics): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2020-09-29 release on Beyoncé & Taylor Swift Lyrics — 22,616 rows after cleaning and merge. How do two era-defining catalogs compare line by line? (culture)
- [BIG MAC INDEX: The Artometrics of Purchasing Power](https://artometrics.com/big-mac-index): A Big Mac costs $8.31 in Switzerland and under $2 in parts of Asia. Twenty years of price data reveal how currency, wages, and exchange rates shape the cost of a burger — and a lot more. (power)
- [BIKETOWN BIKESHARE: The Artometrics of Biketown Bikeshare](https://artometrics.com/biketown-bikeshare): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2018-06-05 release on Biketown Bikeshare — 100,000 rows after cleaning and merge. How long and far do Portland Biketown trips run? (atlas)
- [BILLBOARD HOT 100: The Artometrics of Number-One Domination](https://artometrics.com/billboard-hot-100): Since 1958 the Hot 100 has crowned 1,177 number-one songs. Most lasted two weeks. A few held the top for months. This report counts who dominated, when the longest runs happened, and what has changed since streaming rewrote the rules. (culture)
- [BILLBOARD TOP 100: The Artometrics of Billboard Top 100](https://artometrics.com/billboard-top-100): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2021-09-14 release on Billboard Top 100 — 100,000 rows after cleaning and merge. How did chart positions compress at the top of the Hot 100? (culture)
- [BOARD GAMES: The Artometrics of Board Games](https://artometrics.com/board-games): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2019-03-12 release on Board Games — 10,532 rows after cleaning and merge. Which board games combine crowd scale with elite ratings? (culture)
- [BROADWAY MUSICALS: The Artometrics of Broadway Musicals](https://artometrics.com/broadway-musicals): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2020-04-28 release on Broadway Musicals — 47,524 rows after cleaning and merge. Which musicals ran longest and grossed hardest on Broadway? (culture)
- [CEO DEPARTURES: The Artometrics of CEO Departures](https://artometrics.com/ceo-departures): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2021-04-27 release on CEO Departures — 9,423 rows after cleaning and merge. How long do CEOs last before departure — and how do exits cluster? (power)
- [CETACEANS: The Artometrics of Cetaceans](https://artometrics.com/cetaceans): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2018-12-18 release on Cetaceans — 2,194 rows after cleaning and merge. How do cetacean sizes cluster by family — and who are the giants? (atlas)
- [CHRISTMAS NOVELS: The Artometrics of Christmas Novels](https://artometrics.com/christmas-novels): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2025-12-30 release on Christmas Novels — 35 rows after cleaning and merge. Which Christmas novels and authors anchor the Gutenberg holiday shelf? (culture)
- [CHRISTMAS SONGS: The Artometrics of Christmas Songs](https://artometrics.com/christmas-songs): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2019-12-24 release on Christmas Songs — 387 rows after cleaning and merge. Which holiday standards refuse to leave the chart? (culture)
- [CIA WORLD FACTBOOK: The Artometrics of CIA World Factbook](https://artometrics.com/cia-world-factbook): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2024-10-22 release on CIA World Factbook — 259 rows after cleaning and merge. How do population scale and prosperity relate across countries? (atlas)
- [COLLEGE MAJOR & INCOME: The Artometrics of College Major & Income](https://artometrics.com/college-major-income): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2018-10-16 release on College Major & Income — 173 rows after cleaning and merge. Which majors pay most — and which carry unemployment risk? (power)
- [COMIC BOOK CHARACTERS: The Artometrics of Comic Book Characters](https://artometrics.com/comic-characters): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2018-05-29 release on Comic Book Characters — 23,272 rows after cleaning and merge. Who ranks highest on the power scale — and does size matter? (culture)
- [CRAFT BEER USA: The Artometrics of Craft Beer USA](https://artometrics.com/craft-beer-usa): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2018-07-10 release on Craft Beer USA — 2,410 rows after cleaning and merge. How bitter and strong is American craft beer — by state and brewery? (culture)
- [EMMY AWARDS: The Artometrics of Emmy Awards](https://artometrics.com/emmy-awards): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2021-09-21 release on Emmy Awards — 29,678 rows after cleaning and merge. Which shows dominated Emmy season and how lopsided was the hardware? (culture)
- [EXERCISE USA: The Artometrics of Exercise USA](https://artometrics.com/exercise-usa): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2018-07-17 release on Exercise USA — 52 rows after cleaning and merge. Which states exercise most — and how did activity rates shift? (persona)
- [FAST FOOD CALORIES: The Artometrics of Fast Food Calories](https://artometrics.com/fast-food-calories): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2018-09-04 release on Fast Food Calories — 515 rows after cleaning and merge. Which chains pack the most calories per item? (culture)
- [GLOBAL LIFE EXPECTANCY: How Humanity Doubled Its Lifespan in 500 Years](https://artometrics.com/global-life-expectancy): In 1543 the median human life lasted roughly 30 years. By 2015 it had climbed past 73. This report maps five centuries of survival data — who leads, who lags, and why the gap between them has never been wider. (atlas)
- [GLOBAL PLASTIC WASTE: The Artometrics of Global Plastic Waste](https://artometrics.com/global-plastic-waste): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2019-05-21 release on Global Plastic Waste — 22,204 rows after cleaning and merge. Who generates the most plastic waste — and how much goes mismanaged? (atlas)
- [HORROR MOVIE PROFIT: The Artometrics of Horror Movie Profit](https://artometrics.com/horror-movie-profit): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2018-10-23 release on Horror Movie Profit — 3,401 rows after cleaning and merge. Which horror bets returned multiples and which franchises burned cash? (culture)

- [HURRICANES & PUERTO RICO: The Artometrics of Hurricanes & Puerto Rico](https://artometrics.com/hurricanes-puerto-rico): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2018-06-19 release on Hurricanes & Puerto Rico — 153 rows after cleaning and merge. - [INCARCERATION TRENDS: The Artometrics of Incarceration Trends](https://artometrics.com/incarceration-trends): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2019-01-22 release on Incarceration Trends — 100,000 rows after cleaning and merge. - [WINE RATINGS: The Artometrics of Wine Ratings](https://artometrics.com/wine-ratings): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2019-05-28 release on Wine Ratings — 100,000 rows after cleaning and merge. Does price predict points — or do bargains cluster? (culture)
- [WORLD HERITAGE SITES: The Artometrics of World Heritage Sites](https://artometrics.com/world-heritage-sites): This report analyzes the TidyTuesday 2024-02-06 release on World Heritage Sites — 6 rows after cleaning and merge. How did UNESCO site counts grow across Scandinavia between 2004 and 2022? (atlas)
- [POKEMON: The Artometrics of the World's Biggest Franchise](https://artometrics.com/pokemon): Pokémon is the highest-grossing media franchise in history — more than $150 billion in lifetime revenue across games, cards, merchandise, and film. That number gets cited constantly. What gets cite... (culture)
- [READMITTED: How America's Hospitals Are Failing the 30-Day Standard](https://artometrics.com/readmitted): 11,720 hospital-condition pairs under CMS HRRP: which states, conditions, and ownership types still exceed the 30-day readmission benchmark — and what that means for TEAM in 2026 (power)
- [CAESAR: The Psychonomics of the Great Dictator](https://artometrics.com/caesar-the-psychonomics-of-emperor-julius): Economics comes from the Greek oikonomia — the management of the house. Psychonomics asks the prior question: who is managing the soul of the house, and what does their inner architecture cost ever... (history)
- [GIANT: The Artometrics of A San Francisco Dynasty](https://artometrics.com/giant-the-artometrics-of-a-san-francisco-dynasty): The San Francisco Giants have been playing professional baseball since 1883. That is not a typo. The franchise that now calls Oracle Park home started as the New York Gothams — a team that predates... (atlas)
- [WARRIOR: The Artometrics of A Golden State Dynasty](https://artometrics.com/warrior-the-artometrics-of-a-golden-state-dynasty): The Golden State Warriors have played 78 seasons of professional basketball. For roughly 35 of them, they were irrelevant. Not rebuilding, not transitioning — just losing, year after year, in a market... (atlas)
- [COFFEE: The Artometrics of Java](https://artometrics.com/coffee-the-artometrics-of-java): Coffee is the world's second-largest traded commodity by value, behind only crude oil. More than 125 million people depend on it for their livelihoods. Around 2.25 billion cups are consumed every d... (culture)
- [H3: The Artometrics of A YouTube Dynasty](https://artometrics.com/h3-the-artometrics-of-a-youtube-dynasty): H3 is not one channel — it is a network. Since 2011, Ethan and Hila Klein have built four YouTube channels, one clothing brand, a podcast empire, and one of the most documented creator controversie... (culture)
- [IMPERIAL: The Evolution of Empire](https://artometrics.com/imperial): Every empire in history has told the same story about itself: we brought order, we built roads, we made the trains run on time. What the chronicles leave out is the spreadsheet. Who actually got ri... (history)
- [FRANCHISE: The Artometrics of Media Monopolies](https://artometrics.com/franchise): A data analysis of 107 franchises mapping how the most valuable IP in history — from Pokémon to Star Wars — actually makes its money. (culture)
- [ANIME: The Artometrics of Japanese Animation](https://artometrics.com/anime): 13,631 Titles. A Century Of Data. One Question: What Does The MyAnimeList Archive Actually Tell Us About How Anime Works As An Industry? (culture)

## Podcast

- [Why culture needs a data desk](https://artometrics.com/podcast/interviews/1): Juliet Ramos on treating anime catalogs, streaming libraries, and franchise economics as public evidence — not just fandom.
- [Reading an Artometrics report from scratch](https://artometrics.com/podcast/interviews/2): David Lee walks through observed data, derived metrics, editorial indices, and outside literature — the four evidence types behind every report.
- [Long timelines, small samples: history desk lessons](https://artometrics.com/podcast/interviews/3): Isaac Turner on working with archival gaps, imperial datasets, and the humility required when the past does not arrive in tidy rows.
- [Franchise economics and the persona of a dynasty](https://artometrics.com/podcast/interviews/4): Jordan Wells on measuring sports and creator dynasties — wins, revenue, platform reach, and why myth needs a spreadsheet.

## Generated

Updated: 2026-07-22T19:25:49.149Z
